entry.title: The Gartner IAM Summit - Day 0
entry.summary: I’m in Las Vegas this week for Gartner’s IAM Summit, being held at Ceasar’s Palace. Vegas isn’t really my cup of tea but one goes where the conferences are. Pro tip: The desert is really freaking cold in December. I mean, not Canada cold, but way …
entry.content:
I’m in Las Vegas this week for Gartner’s IAM Summit, being held at Ceasar’s Palace. Vegas isn’t really my cup of tea but one goes where the conferences are. Pro tip: The desert is really freaking cold in December. I mean, not Canada cold, but way colder than a desert should be.

I flew in yesterday, which gave me a leasurely day today for registration, and allowed me to attend a couple “tutorials” as well as the welcome reception. The tutorials were just talks, not apparentyl different from any other conference session I have attended. There was certainly nothing hands-on about them. I peeled out of the welcome reception pretty quick, welcome receptions also not really being my thing. And I do have an exam to study for.

entry.title: Linux Foundation Certified Engineer
entry.summary: Renewal time for my LFCS certification. The Linux Foundation will renew your LFCS certification for 2 years if you upgrade to the LFCE cert, so that’s what I did!
entry.content: Renewal time for my LFCS certification. The Linux Foundation will renew your LFCS certification for 2 years if you upgrade to the LFCE cert, so that’s what I did!

entry.title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate
entry.summary: Another certification in the bag. I wrote the AWS Associate level Solutions Architect exam today. I didn’t realize AWS certs are only good for 2 years. Sets a bit of a roadmap for my AWS certifications going forward. I got my Associate level SysOps cert in earl 2015, so …
entry.content: Another certification in the bag. I wrote the AWS Associate level Solutions Architect exam today. I didn’t realize AWS certs are only good for 2 years. Sets a bit of a roadmap for my AWS certifications going forward. I got my Associate level SysOps cert in earl 2015, so I’ll want to replace it with the Professional level DevOps cert next year, and I’ll want to replace my Associate level Architect cert with the Professional level cert in 2018.

entry.title: Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator
entry.summary: I’ve been at this Unix and Linux systems management stuff for over 20 years now. As with my undergraduate degree1, I’ve long wanted to knock out a few relevant technology certifications. Following on from my AWS SysOps certification, I took and passed the Linux Foundation’s LFCS …
entry.content:
I’ve been at this Unix and Linux systems management stuff for over 20 years now. As with my undergraduate degree1, I’ve long wanted to knock out a few relevant technology certifications. Following on from my AWS SysOps certification, I took and passed the Linux Foundation’s LFCS certification exam this week.

  1. I am a part-time undergrad at my local University, chipping away at a B.A. in Economics. ↩

entry.title: AWS SysOps Administration - Associate
entry.summary: Last week I took the AWS SysOps Administrator - Associate test and passed with flying colours. Next step will be the Developer and then the DevOps certifications, both of which I hope to complete this year. I look forward to doing a lot more work in AWS in the future.
entry.content: Last week I took the AWS SysOps Administrator - Associate test and passed with flying colours. Next step will be the Developer and then the DevOps certifications, both of which I hope to complete this year. I look forward to doing a lot more work in AWS in the future.

entry.title: My First Kaggle Competition
entry.summary:
We are wrapping up the final week of MIT’s 15.071x “The Analytics Edge” course, delivered online via edX.org.

As part of the course I participated in my first Kaggle competition. I finished in 1406th place out of 2923 people with a Private leaderboard score of 0.88319 …
entry.content:
We are wrapping up the final week of MIT’s 15.071x “The Analytics Edge” course, delivered online via edX.org.

As part of the course I participated in my first Kaggle competition. I finished in 1406th place out of 2923 people with a Private leaderboard score of 0.88319, down from a public leaderboard score of 0.91792.

The mean score for all submissions was 0.8551523, median 0.90237, with a standard deviation of 0.09301734.

Min.    1st Qu. Median  Mean    3rd Qu. Max.
0.2500  0.7945  0.9024  0.8552  0.9204  0.9455

Not a great result, but a great learning experience. Many of the top finishers are sharing their code so I look forward to reviewing what they did. It is interesting to see some of the dramatic shifts in the top tier finishers when going from the publicly available test data to the private test data set.

My code is here.

The red and blue lines below represent my final and public leaderboard scores respectively.
